combos.notes: Starting with 5LK instead of 2LK is 1 frame faster and adds 100 damage Starting with 2LP instead of 2LK is 1 frame slower and adds 100 damage Standard Light BNB Electric Thunder gives +39F on hit to keep pressure and is kind of safe with -3F on block OD: +140 damage, +40F on hit, +4F on block, SA2/SA3 cancelable / Standard Light BNB OD: +140 damage, +40F on hit, +4F on block, SA2/SA3 cancelable / Standard Light BNB Rolling Attack is more damaging and resets to neutral, but extremely unsafe if blocked

4MK is your short-range, plus-frame friend More so a string, has 2 frame traps and is safe on block. Punishes mashing in case it's blocked. Be aware of parries 214PP cancels into SA2/3
4MK is your short-range, plus-frame friend
4MK is your short-range, plus-frame friend If you hit confirm 4LP, re-route into this for extra damage, but less Oki
4MK is your short-range, plus-frame friend 214PP has a very generous early cancel for 236236K, no need to time it

BNB route from 4MK. Electric is less damage but better to keep pressure 214PP is cancelable into SA2 for extended combo / BNB route from 4MK. Electric is less damage but better to keep pressure These all can be done with 2MK instead of 5MK, but less damage / BNB route from 4MK. Electric is less damage but better to keep pressure Never go for Rolling Attack without confirm, it is -23F on block Rolling Attack has to be done off 1MK because of charge / BNB route from 4MK. Electric is less damage but better to keep pressure Rolling Attack has to be done off 1MK because of charge
Not worth it! Just go for the previous line and save 2 bars

THE standard Heavy BNB. Often in longer combos where 5HP can be confirmed. 2PP~P has numerous potential follow-ups that can be utilized, including Blanka Doll setups, so become comfortable this route. / THE standard Heavy BNB. Often in longer combos where 5HP can be confirmed. Just keep holding 2 on Coward Crouch to charge [2]8MK If 5HP hit at max. range, [2]8MK is probably going to whiff, do [2]8LK instead (-80 damage) / THE standard Heavy BNB. Often in longer combos where 5HP can be confirmed. If 5HP hit at max. range, [2]8MK is probably going to whiff, do [2]8LK instead (-80 damage)

You can Drive Rush Cancel into itself for an instant burnout The only moment that is going to be worth instantly burning yourself out like this is going for a K.O.
You can Drive Rush Cancel into itself for an instant burnout This. Maximum damage without any extra shenanigans.
Example of corner routes after guard crush. In this case, Vertical Rolling Attack won't hit after 63214KK

3-Drive conversion from a Rainbow Ball cross-up.
Standard cross-up confirm for when hitting the opponent as high/early as possible.
Cross-up confirm when hitting close to the ground or on crouching opponents, allowing standard HP routing.
Cross-up confirm when hitting close to the ground or on crouching opponents, allowing standard HP routing.
Drive Impact Starter examples. Can tack on whatever ender you'd like w/ the right amount of meter, including SA2 if you choose to OD Electric.
Wild Lift has numerous follow-up options and setups, so experiment.
Punish combo extension from your standard Blanka Ball. 1-Drive for an extra 700 damage is great value, so get used to using this on reaction to a punish counter.

Light punish combo examples. 1st is consistent at all ranges, the other two utilize drive for consistency and juiced damage, great for many attacks which are -4/-6. Application will require matchup knowledge.

-7 punish that happens to be your standard drive rush extension. As HK is only -2 and has a large animation for hit-confirming, having this as a hit-confirm alongside 5HK,5LP is pretty useful should you occasionally toss out 5HK in your pressure.
Anti-Air counter-hit combo routes. Enemy is propelled upward after counter-hit, allowing some follow-ups even after a trade. Just a few examples of what's possible.
Anti-Air counter-hit combo routes. Enemy is propelled upward after counter-hit, allowing some follow-ups even after a trade. Just a few examples of what's possible.

Overhead combo from Counter-hit and Punish Counter respectively.
Overhead combo from Counter-hit and Punish Counter respectively.
Punishes for blocked reversals. Drive Rush can be used to guarantee the 5HP extension, but this will prorate the combo and cost drive meter which you may rather avoid.
[4]6PP route is easier and more flexible (Numerous capable routings after [4]6PP leaves them airborne) at the cost of 2-drive but forces side-swap.
Punish for blocked reversals near the corner. If the opponent is close to the corner, the dash is unnecessary, making the route much easier.
3-Drive for a reliable, on-demand whiff punish route with numerous options left open for you after Wild Lift. Worth learning. / +3-4k damage can be easily tacked on with SA1-3 use.
Similar to the above, but from 5LP/2LP which have above average range for Light Attacks. / Good for possible spacing traps such as after a blocked Electric.
An easier alternative from 5LP/2LP utilizing the standard light drive rush combo. / Use if you cannot confirm the Punish Counter as this still leads to pressure/damage.
Buffer super behind either MP for a quick and easy whiff punish requiring no Drive. Can also be used as a basic poke/counter-poke, but be careful as SA 1 and 3 are very punishable if blocked.
On the odd chance you have a charge ready when you whiff punish and really want to save 1 drive meter.

Your BNB. DR>6MK is an easy to confirm button due to its length and can be used as a meaty in some situations. / If it lands, opt for any 5HP routing you want. / Alternatively DR>4MK gives enormous frame advantage, making it a decent/safer option for extending pressure by simply looping into itself as shown in the combo below.

Must be point-blank to the opponent: Easier to confirm due to repetition, HAS TO be reversed or parried. Can frame trap. / No Hit-Confirm: Use drive to 214PP for +4F and continue pressure if blocked. / Hit Confirm: Use drive to extend a combo with a [4]6PP Route.

Must be point-blank to the opponent: Easier to confirm due to repetition, HAS TO be reversed or parried. Can frame trap. / No Hit-Confirm: Use drive to 214PP for +4F and continue pressure if blocked. / Hit Confirm: Use drive to extend a combo with a [4]6PP Route.

Must be point-blank to the opponent: Easier to confirm due to repetition, HAS TO be reversed or parried. Can frame trap. / No Hit-Confirm: Use drive to 214PP for +4F and continue pressure if blocked. / Hit Confirm: Use drive to extend a combo with a [4]6PP Route.

Must be point-blank to the opponent: Easier to confirm due to repetition, HAS TO be reversed or parried. Can frame trap. / No Hit-Confirm: Use drive to 214PP for +4F and continue pressure if blocked. / Hit Confirm: Use drive to extend a combo with a [4]6PP Route.
The easier route to learn and less input heavy option, can still chain after a block if opponent still holding high block.
Route with 5LK frame trap to utilize if you have a hard time confirming the overhead hit and suspect the opponent will mash after blocking.
Overhead Drive Rush BNB.
Low Drive Rush BNB to mix in with your overhead. You'll want to be fairly close when landing the initial hit;
A Blanka Doll confirm similar to the first, with increased damage and extra time to confirm or extend pressure if blocked.
